refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load→travel→dump cycle.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR specifies a single functional obligation: to support achieving a defined cycle time target for a specific operational cycle. It is already focused on what the system must do (support achieving the cycle time target) without specifying how, thus it is solution-neutral and atomic.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the cycle time for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, and verify that the 95th percentile (p95) cycle time is less than or equal to the customer-defined target (CT_target).
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load→travel→dump cycle.

candidate_dps: Optimize load handling mechanism to minimize load time
Optimize travel mechanism to minimize travel time
Optimize dump mechanism to minimize dump time
Coordinate load, travel, and dump operations to minimize total cycle time
Implement cycle time monitoring and adaptive control to meet target
selected_dp.selected_dp: Coordinate load, travel, and dump operations to minimize total cycle time

potential_sources: Interdependence between load, travel, and dump operations timing
coupling_check.notes: Selecting coordination of all operations as a single DP helps manage dependencies and optimize overall cycle time, reducing coupling by treating the cycle holistically rather than optimizing sub-operations independently.

assumptions_risks: Assumes that coordination can be effectively implemented without excessive complexity
Risk that external factors (e.g., site conditions) may impact cycle time beyond control of coordination
Assumes accurate measurement and control of cycle time components
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly decomposing the DP into sub-DPs for load, travel, and dump optimization if coordination complexity becomes unmanageable, ensuring modularity and maintainability.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Coordinates load, travel, and dump operations to minimize total cycle time across the system

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Exchanges operational data for coordination and monitoring

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands and task assignments for operation coordination

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Monitors and communicates safety-related alerts during operations

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports communication between distributed modules and external systems

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as it is the top-level system coordinating operations; minimal diffusion since coordination logic is centralized here.
Partial containment possible but coordination across multiple vehicles and operations would cause significant diffusion.
Focuses on remote control aspects, not overall operation coordination; poor containment.
Primarily safety monitoring, not operation coordination; very poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the DP because it encompasses the overall system coordination required to optimize load, travel, and dump operations, minimizing cycle time effectively with minimal diffusion outside this boundary.
containment_risks: Potential complexity in the NGHE Solution module due to broad coordination responsibilities.
Risk of tight coupling with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules if interfaces are not well defined.
Possible performance bottlenecks if coordination logic is centralized without adequate distribution.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: No revision recommended; the DP is best contained within the NGHE Solution module as currently defined to maintain clear ownership and minimize diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by specifying coordination of load, travel, and dump operations to minimize total cycle time, directly supporting the customer-defined cycle time target. This mapping is clear and grounded in the acceptance criterion of achieving p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target.

diffusion_risks: Potential complexity in the NGHE Solution module due to broad coordination responsibilities.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating the DP is well centralized with minimal scattering across modules. Other modules have lower containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the IM boundary to minimize diffusion risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through measuring cycle time performance (p95 ≤ CT_target) under defined duty cycles and site conditions, as well as monitoring operational policies and safety plans via the SafetyAlert interface.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear, with well-defined external interfaces supporting coordination and monitoring, ensuring traceability from FR through DP to IM.

recommended_revisions: No revision recommended; the DP is best contained within the NGHE Solution module as currently defined to maintain clear ownership and minimize diffusion.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and no recommended revisions. The DP logically addresses the FR with clear acceptance criteria, the DP is well contained within the NGHE Solution IM module minimizing diffusion risk, and verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear, centralized module boundary is essential and currently achieved. Therefore, no further decomposition is necessary at this time.

assumptions: The current DP fully satisfies the customer-defined cycle time target and safety requirements.
The NGHE Solution module can handle the coordination responsibilities without excessive complexity.
Verification methods for cycle time and safety monitoring are sufficient and reliable.
open_risks: Potential complexity in the NGHE Solution module due to broad coordination responsibilities remains a monitoring point but is mitigated by high containment and low diffusion scores.
evidence_needed: Operational data confirming p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target under defined duty cycles and site conditions.
Verification reports on SafetyAlert interface effectiveness and policy monitoring.
